explanation: The property states that globally, whenever the system is in 'Start-up Mode', the 'loadLog' condition must be true. This captures the requirement that logged key parameters shall be loaded upon start-up, implying that during the start-up phase, these parameters are being loaded or are available.

The property states that globally, whenever the start-up process of the system is successfully completed (represented by "startUpDone"), it must eventually be the case ("F") that the system loads the key parameters (represented by "loadLog") and simultaneously, the GUI is connected (represented by "guiConnected"), thereby making the parameters available on the GUI's log page.

The property specifies that whenever the system enters 'StartUpMode' (i.e., 'StartUpMode' was false and becomes true), then 'loadLog' must eventually occur. It also covers the initial state: if the system is already in 'StartUpMode' at time 0, then 'loadLog' must eventually occur. The 'loadLog' atomic proposition is interpreted to include the parameters being made available on the GUI, as no more specific atomic proposition is available.

This LTL property specifies that globally, whenever the system/controller initialization starts (initStart), the logged key parameters will eventually be loaded (loadLog). This captures the requirement that the loading of parameters is a guaranteed outcome upon system start-up.

The property states that upon the successful completion of the system's start-up (i.e., when 'startUpDone' transitions from false to true), the logged key parameters shall be loaded and made available ('loadLog') in the immediate next state. Furthermore, for as long as 'startUpDone' remains true, it is ensured that the parameters remain in a loaded/available state, fulfilling the requirement for them to be continuously 'made available on a log page on the GUI'.

The property states that whenever the system transitions into 'StartUpMode' (representing 'upon start-up'), 'loadLog' (representing 'load logged key parameters') must be true in the next state. Additionally, it ensures that for as long as the system is in 'StartUpMode', 'loadLog' remains true, indicating that the parameters are continually available on the GUI log page throughout the start-up phase.

The property ensures that whenever the system is in its 'StartUpMode', the 'loadLog' condition (indicating that logged key parameters are loaded and made available) must be true. This directly translates the requirement that "The system shall load logged key parameters upon start-up to be made available on a log page on the GUI" by mapping 'upon start-up' to the 'StartUpMode' and 'load logged key parameters to be made available' to 'loadLog'.

The property states that globally, whenever the system initialization starts ('initStart'), then in the very next state, the system must have loaded the parameters ('loadLog') and the GUI must be connected ('guiConnected'), which implies the parameters are made available on the GUI.

The LTL property ensures that whenever the system initiates 'StartUpMode', it will eventually load the logged parameters and make them available on the GUI within a specified duration (N_DURATION). The first part of the formula, 'G (((! StartUpMode) & (X StartUpMode)) -> (X (F[0,N_DURATION] (loadLog & guiConnected))))', captures the requirement for any transition into 'StartUpMode'. The second part, '(StartUpMode -> (F[0,N_DURATION] (loadLog & guiConnected)))', ensures that if the system is already in 'StartUpMode' at the initial state, the condition is still met.

The LTL property states that globally (always), if the system is in 'Start-up Mode', which corresponds to 'upon start-up', then 'logged key parameters' are loaded ('loadLog') AND the 'GUI is connected' ('guiConnected'), ensuring they are made available on a log page on the GUI. This captures that the loading and availability should occur as long as the system is in the start-up phase.
